chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, AT KARACHI Cr. Misc. Appln. No. 500 of 2022. Cr. Misc. Appln. No. 501 of 2022. ______________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of judge Disposed of matter. For orders on M.A No. 12972 of 2025. 22.09.2025. M/s Qadir Hussain Khan and Shehla Anjum, advocates for the applicant. Mr. Siraj Ahmed Khan Chandio, Addl. P.G. ======= For grounds mentioned in the listed applications as well as affidavits, both these criminal miscellaneous applications are restored to their original stage. These are two criminal miscellaneous applications No.500 and 501 of 2022 out of which one is in respect of FIR No.689 of 2020 of P.S Sohrab Goth lodged by P.I SHO Younus Khattak posted at New Karachi Industrial Area and the other for FIR No.826 of 2020 of P.S Sohrab Goth lodged by Aamir Ali S/O Qasim Abro wherein cognizance has been taken, while the FIR lodged by SHO Younus Khattak has been disposed of vide report of I.O under C Class and such report has been approved by learned Judicial Magistrate through its order dated 04.12.2021. When confronted with the impugned order dated 13.8.2021 passed by learned 1st Additional Sessions Judge Malir Karachi in Sessions Case No. 2627 of 2021 that the reason for cognizance duly incorporated as statement under Section 164 Cr.P.C has been recorded and learned Magistrate was satisfied with the report of the I.O and he has transmitted case to the learned Sessions Judge in compliance of Section 190 Cr.P.C, learned counsel does not press this criminal miscellaneous application and request for directions may be given to the trial Court to decide the case within shortest possible period.

These are two criminal miscellaneous applications No.500 and 501 of 2022 out of which one is in respect of FIR No.689 of 2020 of P.S Sohrab Goth lodged by P.I SHO Younus Khattak posted at New Karachi Industrial Area and the other for FIR No.826 of 2020 of P.S Sohrab Goth lodged by Aamir Ali S/O Qasim Abro wherein cognizance has been taken, while the FIR lodged by SHO Younus Khattak has been disposed of vide report of I.O under C Class and such report has been approved by learned Judicial Magistrate through its order dated 04.12.2021. When confronted with the impugned order dated 13.8.2021 passed by learned 1st Additional Sessions Judge Malir Karachi in Sessions Case No. 2627 of 2021 that the reason for cognizance duly incorporated as statement under Section 164 Cr.P.C has been recorded and learned Magistrate was satisfied with the report of the I.O and he has transmitted case to the learned Sessions Judge in compliance of Section 190 Cr.P.C, learned counsel does not press this criminal miscellaneous application and request for directions may be given to the trial Court to decide the case within shortest possible period. Accordingly both these criminal miscellaneous applications are dismissed as not pressed with direction to the trial Court to proceed with the evidence and decide the case preferably within a period of two months. JUDGE Shabir/P.S
